title: Selinas the Chefs Falling on the Tree

content.problemStatement:
You are given a tree with NNN nodes numbered 111 through NNN, rooted at node 111. There are many really good ingredients on this tree, so MMM chefs Selinas are going to pick them all up. For each iii (1≤i≤M1 \le i \le M1≤i≤M), the iii-th Selina will start falling from the root in the tit_iti​-th second.

Obviously, Selinas can only move in two directions — from each node, a Selina can fall to one of its children or bounce to its parent. When bouncing, there is only one path to take, but when falling, the Selina will choose the node to fall down to uniformly randomly among all children of the current node. One move in either direction takes one second.

Each Selina only changes her direction (from bouncing to falling or vice versa) when she cannot keep moving in the current direction without leaving the tree or hitting another Selina, that is, when one of the following happens:


reaching a leaf when falling
reaching the root when bouncing
meeting another Selina that's moving in the opposite direction
At the QQQ-th second, we would like to know the expected number of Selinas that have passed through each node so far. Formally, let's denote the expected number of Selinas in node iii at the jjj-th second by Ei,jE_{i, j}Ei,j​; for each node iii, you should find the sum of Ei,jE_{i, j}Ei,j​ over all seconds jjj between 111 and QQQ (inclusive). Compute these numbers modulo 998244353998244353998244353.
constraints: Subtasks**Subtask #1 (50 points):** the tree is generated randomly
**Subtask #2 (10 points):** for each valid iii, the parent of node iii is node (i−1)(i-1)(i−1)
**Subtask #3 (10 points):** the parent of each non-root node is the root
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer NNN.
Each of the following N−1N-1N−1 lines contains two space-separated integers uuu and vvv denoting an edge between nodes uuu and vvv.
The next line contains a single integer MMM.
The next line contains MMM space-separated integers t1,t2,…,tMt_1, t_2, \dots, t_Mt1​,t2​,…,tM​ denoting the times when the Selinas start to fall from the root.
The last line contains a single integer QQQ.
content.outputFormat: Print a single line containing NNN space-separated integers. For each valid iii, the iii-th of these integers should denote the sum of expected numbers of Selinas that have been in the iii-th node in seconds 111 through QQQ (modulo 998244353998244353998244353).

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
On second 111, the only Selina can only be in the node 111. Then, on second 222, this Selina must fall either to node 222 or to node 333, with probability 1/21/21/2 each. Overall, the expected number of Selinas that have been in node 111 in seconds 111 through 222 is 111 and the expected number for nodes 222 and 333 is 1/2≡4991221771/2 \equiv 4991221771/2≡499122177 modulo 998244353998244353998244353.
